scripts.package: bun run build && pkg . --sea --compress GZip --out-path dist
scripts.package:win: bun run build && pkg . --sea --targets node22-win-x64 --compress GZip --out-path dist
scripts.package:mac: bun run build && pkg . --sea --targets node22-macos-x64,node22-macos-arm64 --compress GZip --out-path dist
scripts.package:mac-arm64: bun run build && pkg . --sea --targets node22-macos-arm64 --compress GZip --out-path dist
scripts.package:mac-x64: bun run build && pkg . --sea --targets node22-macos-x64 --compress GZip --out-path dist
scripts.package:linux: bun run build && pkg . --sea --targets node22-linux-x64 --compress GZip --out-path dist

//: - `scripts` lists JS files to bake into the pkg snapshot in
addition to whatever the launcher require()s. We point at the
full SvelteKit Node server output so every chunk is bundled,
not just the entrypoint module's static dependencies.
- `assets` lists *non-JS* files (HTML, CSS, fonts, images, the
yt-dlp binary) that need to be readable at runtime. pkg only
bundles the assets you list — it can't infer them from
`fs.readFileSync` calls — so we glob the entire client/server
- `targets` is omitted on purpose: the value is supplied at the
command line via the `package:*` npm scripts so we can build
Windows / macOS / Linux artifacts independently from CI.
